heading: Eligibility of California Indians

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1679, , formerly title VII, § 709, as added , , ; amended , , ; renumbered title VIII, § 809, , (b), , , related to report to Congress containing sufficient data to determine eligibility of California Indians for health services provided by the Service and preliminary eligibility criteria, prior to repeal by , , . children.text: Any member of a federally recognized Indian tribe.

children.label: A
children.text: is a member of the Indian community served by a local program of the Service; and

children.label: B
children.text: is regarded as an Indian by the community in which such descendant lives.
Any Indian who holds trust interests in public domain, national forest, or reservation allotments in California.

Any Indian of California who is listed on the plans for distribution of the assets of rancherias and reservations located within the State of California under the Act of (), and any descendant of such an Indian.August 18, 195872 Stat. 619

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: b Clarification
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed as expanding the eligibility of California Indians for health services provided by the Service beyond the scope of eligibility for such health services that applied on .May 1, 1986
